source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure '3625 (trace flag)' Database Flag for all Cloud SQL SQL Server Instances Is Set to 'on'

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure '3625 (trace flag)' Database Flag for all Cloud SQL SQL Server Instances Is Set to 'on'
input_row.description: It is recommended to set 3625 (trace flag) database flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server instance to on.
input_row.rationale: Microsoft SQL Trace Flags are frequently used to diagnose performance issues or to debug stored procedures or complex computer systems, but they may also be recommended by Microsoft Support to address behavior that is negatively impacting a specific workload. All documented trace flags and those recommended by Microsoft Support are fully supported in a production environment when used as directed. 3625(trace log) Limits the amount of information returned to users who are not members of the sysadmin fixed server role, by masking the parameters of some error messages using '******'. Setting this in a Google Cloud flag for the instance allows for security through obscurity and prevents the disclosure of sensitive information, hence this is recommended to set this flag globally to on to prevent the flag having been left off, or changed by bad actors. This recommendation is applicable to SQL Server database instances. Impact: Changing flags on a database may cause it to be restarted. The best time to do this is at a time where there is low usage.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page 3. Ensure the database flag 3625 that has been set is listed under the Database flags section. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Ensure the below command returns on for every Cloud SQL SQL Server database instance gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="3625")|.value'
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the SQL Server instance for which you want to enable to database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag 3625 from the drop-down menu, and set its value to on. 6. Click Save to save your changes. 7. Confirm your changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the 3625 database flag for every Cloud SQL SQL Server database instance using the below command. gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags "3625=on" Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags you want set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: MS SQL Server implementations by default have trace flags, including the '3625' flag, turned off, as they are used for logging purposes. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/database-console-commands/dbcc- traceon-trace-flags-transact-sql?view=sql-server-ver15#trace-flags 3. https://github.com/ktaranov/sqlserver- kit/blob/master/SQL%20Server%20Trace%20Flag.md Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag restarts the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Changing flags on a database may cause it to be restarted. The best time to do this is at a time where there is low usage.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/database-console-commands/dbcc- traceon-trace-flags-transact-sql?view=sql-server-ver15#trace-flags 3. https://github.com/ktaranov/sqlserver- kit/blob/master/SQL%20Server%20Trace%20Flag.md Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag restarts the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit can be fully automated due to the following reasons:
- GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) can query and update database flags, including the specific trace flag 3625 for Cloud SQL SQL Server instances.
- The audit field provides a gcloud command to verify if the database flag 3625 is set to "on".
- This is a standard GCP service property related to database configuration, which can be queried and modified using the GCP CLI.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_trace_flag_3625_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) CLI command to enumerate all Cloud SQL SQL Server instances.
- Execute the following GCP CLI command for each Cloud SQL SQL Server instance to verify the database flag 3625 is set to "on":
  ```
  gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="3625")|.value'
  ```
- Check if the returned value is "on".
- Pass Criteria: The command returns "on" for every Cloud SQL SQL Server instance.
- Fail Criteria: The command does not return "on" or the flag 3625 is not set for any instance.
- This automated check should be run across all regions where Cloud SQL SQL Server instances are deployed.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. This control can be fully checked via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). There is no need for manual intervention unless automation fails or there are edge cases.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud sql instances describe` command exposes all database flags for Cloud SQL SQL Server instances. The `databaseFlags` property is available in the API response.
- **GCP CLI/SDK/Config:**  
  The check is feasible via CLI and can be scripted for all instances and regions. GCP Config (now called Config Connector) does not natively monitor this flag, so CLI/API is the correct approach.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The command `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="3625")|.value'` is technically correct for extracting the value of the flag.
  - However, the flag for SQL Server trace flags is typically set as a database flag with the name `traceflags` and a comma-separated list of enabled flags (e.g., `traceflags=3625`). The flag name is not simply `"3625"`, but rather `"traceflags"` with a value that should include `"3625"`.
  - The current approach will **not work** as written because there is no database flag named `"3625"`; instead, you should check if `"3625"` is present in the value of the `"traceflags"` flag.
- **Program Name:**  
  - The program name `gcp_sql_instance_trace_flag_3625_enabled` is reasonably descriptive, but could be more precise (see Improvements).
- **Completeness:**  
  - The approach does not mention filtering for only SQL Server instances (`--database-version` filter).
  - It does not handle the case where `databaseFlags` is empty or missing.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Correct Flag Name and Value Extraction:**  
  - The correct flag to check is `traceflags`, not `3625`.
  - The value of `traceflags` is a comma-separated string (e.g., `"3625,1234"`). The check should parse this string and look for `"3625"` as a discrete value.
  - Example improved command:
    ```bash
    gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format="json" | \
      jq -r '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="traceflags") | .value' | \
      grep -qw 3625
    ```
    Or, in Python or Bash, split the value on commas and check for `3625`.
- **Instance Filtering:**  
  - Only SQL Server instances should be checked. Use `gcloud sql instances list --filter="databaseVersion:SQLSERVER"` to enumerate relevant instances.
- **Region Coverage:**  
  - The assessment mentions "all regions," but Cloud SQL is a regional resource. Ensure the script enumerates all instances across the project, not just a default region.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - For clarity and convention, use:  
    `gcp_sql_instance_traceflags_3625_enabled`  
    or  
    `gcp_sql_sqlserver_instance_traceflag_3625_enabled`
- **Edge Cases:**  
  - The script should handle instances with no `databaseFlags` set (i.e., missing key).
  - Should explicitly document that the check is only for SQL Server, not MySQL or PostgreSQL.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **MEDIUM**

- The general approach is correct and feasible with GCP APIs/CLI, but the technical implementation is flawed due to misunderstanding of the flag structure.
- With the suggested improvements, this can be a robust automated control.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Check for the `traceflags` flag and parse its value for `3625`.
  - Filter only SQL Server instances.
  - Update the program name for clarity.
  - Handle empty/missing `databaseFlags`.
- **Do not** reclassify as Manual; this is still an Automated control with the correct implementation.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section             | Result/Score | Notes                                                                                          |
|---------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE        | Automated is correct; feasible via GCP CLI/API.                                                |
| Technical Accuracy  | 8/10         | Command logic flawed; flag name/value extraction needs correction.                             |
| Improvements        | -            | Check `traceflags`, filter SQL Server, handle edge cases, better program name.                 |
| Confidence          | MEDIUM       | Approach is sound, but technical details need correction.                                      |
| Final Recommendation| MODIFY       | Update automation details and program name as per above; keep as Automated.                    |

---
